role: A React toggle chip that selects vector-lens resolution (full dimension or truncated powers-of-two) for a meaning-zoom control.
capabilities: Fetch active layer full vector dimensions from API
Derive resolution ladder as powers of two strictly below the full dim
Render a dropdown resolution picker with current selection
Update the active dimension via callback prop
Conditionally hide on non-vector lenses or when no finer step exists
substance_why: It implements the complete control: data fetching, option derivation, menu interactions, conditional visibility, and animation.
interactions_present: Click chip button to toggle the menu
Click scrim to close the menu
Click a resolution option to set the dimension and close the menu
interactions_missing: Focus management / focus trap inside the dropdown
Loading or error feedback for the layer-dims fetch
Disabled / aria-selected states for individual options
performance_risks: Ladder and options arrays are recomputed on every render without memoization
Layer dimensions are fetched on every component mount even if shared across instances
Framer-motion dropdown animations may cause layout work
reuse_why: The resolution-picker concept and ladder logic are domain-specific and worth keeping, but the presentation and menu shell should be rebuilt with the new design system.
decomposition_hints: Extract ladder derivation and layer-dims fetching into a reusable hook or data layer
Split the menu shell (button, scrim, animated list) into a generic dropdown primitive
Separate resolution-specific option rendering and selection logic from the generic menu component
